api_types.name: JobCondition
api_types.doc: JobCondition describes the state of the job at a certain point.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Type of job condition.

fields.name: Status
fields.doc: Status of the condition, one of True, False, Unknown.

fields.name: Reason
fields.doc: The reason for the condition's last transition.

fields.name: Message
fields.doc: A human readable message indicating details about the transition.

fields.name: LastUpdateTime
fields.doc: The last time this condition was updated.

fields.name: LastTransitionTime
fields.doc: Last time the condition transitioned from one status to another.

api_types.name: RunPolicy
api_types.doc: RunPolicy encapsulates various runtime policies of the distributed training job, for example how to clean up resources and how long the job can stay active.

fields.name: CleanPodPolicy
fields.doc: CleanPodPolicy defines the policy to kill pods after the job completes. Default to None.

fields.name: TTLSecondsAfterFinished
fields.doc: TTLSecondsAfterFinished is the TTL to clean up jobs. It may take extra ReconcilePeriod seconds for the cleanup, since reconcile gets called periodically. Default to infinite.

fields.name: ActiveDeadlineSeconds
fields.doc: Specifies the duration in seconds relative to the startTime that the job may be active before the system tries to terminate it; value must be positive integer.

fields.name: BackoffLimit
fields.doc: Optional number of retries before marking this job failed.

fields.name: SchedulingPolicy
fields.doc: SchedulingPolicy defines the policy related to scheduling, e.g. gang-scheduling

fields.name: Suspend
fields.doc: suspend specifies whether the Job controller should create Pods or not. If a Job is created with suspend set to true, no Pods are created by the Job controller. If a Job is suspended after creation (i.e. the flag goes from false to true), the Job controller will delete all active Pods and PodGroups associated with this Job. Users must design their workload to gracefully handle this. Suspending a Job will reset the StartTime field of the Job. Defaults to false.

fields.name: ManagedBy
fields.doc: ManagedBy is used to indicate the controller or entity that manages a job. The value must be either an empty, 'kubeflow.org/training-operator' or 'kueue.x-k8s.io/multikueue'. The training-operator reconciles a job which doesn't have this field at all or the field value is the reserved string 'kubeflow.org/training-operator', but delegates reconciling the job with 'kueue.x-k8s.io/multikueue' to the Kueue. The field is immutable.

api_types.name: SchedulingPolicy
api_types.doc: SchedulingPolicy encapsulates various scheduling policies of the distributed training job, for example `minAvailable` for gang-scheduling.

api_types.name: JobStatus
api_types.doc: JobStatus represents the current observed state of the training Job.

fields.name: Conditions
fields.doc: Conditions is an array of current observed job conditions.

fields.name: ReplicaStatuses
fields.doc: ReplicaStatuses is map of ReplicaType and ReplicaStatus, specifies the status of each replica.

fields.name: StartTime
fields.doc: Represents time when the job was acknowledged by the job controller. It is not guaranteed to be set in happens-before order across separate operations. It is represented in RFC3339 form and is in UTC.

fields.name: CompletionTime
fields.doc: Represents time when the job was completed. It is not guaranteed to be set in happens-before order across separate operations. It is represented in RFC3339 form and is in UTC.

fields.name: LastReconcileTime
fields.doc: Represents last time when the job was reconciled. It is not guaranteed to be set in happens-before order across separate operations. It is represented in RFC3339 form and is in UTC.

api_types.name: ReplicaStatus
api_types.doc: ReplicaStatus represents the current observed state of the replica.

fields.name: Active
fields.doc: The number of actively running pods.

fields.name: Succeeded
fields.doc: The number of pods which reached phase Succeeded.

fields.name: Failed
fields.doc: The number of pods which reached phase Failed.

fields.name: LabelSelector
fields.doc: Deprecated: Use Selector instead

fields.name: Selector
fields.doc: A Selector is a label query over a set of resources. The result of matchLabels and matchExpressions are ANDed. An empty Selector matches all objects. A null Selector matches no objects.

api_types.name: ReplicaSpec
api_types.doc: ReplicaSpec is a description of the replica

fields.name: Replicas
fields.doc: Replicas is the desired number of replicas of the given template. If unspecified, defaults to 1.

fields.name: Template
fields.doc: Template is the object that describes the pod that will be created for this replica. RestartPolicy in PodTemplateSpec will be overide by RestartPolicy in ReplicaSpec

fields.name: RestartPolicy
fields.doc: Restart policy for all replicas within the job. One of Always, OnFailure, Never and ExitCode. Default to Never.

api_types.name: JAXJob
api_types.doc: JAXJob Represents a JAXJob resource.

fields.name: TypeMeta
fields.doc: Standard Kubernetes type metadata.

fields.name: Spec
fields.doc: Specification of the desired state of the JAXJob.

fields.name: Status
fields.doc: Most recently observed status of the JAXJob. Read-only (modified by the system).

api_types.name: JAXJobSpec
api_types.doc: JAXJobSpec is a desired state description of the JAXJob.

fields.name: RunPolicy
fields.doc: RunPolicy encapsulates various runtime policies of the distributed training job, for example how to clean up resources and how long the job can stay active.

fields.name: JAXReplicaSpecs
fields.doc: A map of JAXReplicaType (type) to ReplicaSpec (value). Specifies the JAX cluster configuration. For example, { "Worker": JAXReplicaSpec, }

api_types.name: JAXJobList
api_types.doc: JAXJobList is a list of JAXJobs.

fields.name: TypeMeta
fields.doc: Standard type metadata.

fields.name: ListMeta
fields.doc: Standard list metadata.

fields.name: Items
fields.doc: List of JAXJobs.

fields.name: SlotsPerWorker
fields.doc: Specifies the number of slots per worker used in hostfile. Defaults to 1.

fields.name: CleanPodPolicy
fields.doc: CleanPodPolicy defines the policy that whether to kill pods after the job completes. Defaults to None.

fields.name: MPIReplicaSpecs
fields.doc: `MPIReplicaSpecs` contains maps from `MPIReplicaType` to `ReplicaSpec` that specify the MPI replicas to run.

fields.name: MainContainer
fields.doc: MainContainer specifies name of the main container which executes the MPI code.

fields.name: RunPolicy
fields.doc: `RunPolicy` encapsulates various runtime policies of the distributed training job, for example how to clean up resources and how long the job can stay active.

api_types.name: PaddleJobList
api_types.doc: PaddleJobList is a list of PaddleJobs.

fields.name: TypeMeta
fields.doc: Standard type metadata.

fields.name: ListMeta
fields.doc: Standard list metadata.

fields.name: Items
fields.doc: List of PaddleJobs.

api_types.name: PaddleJob
api_types.doc: PaddleJob Represents a PaddleJob resource.

fields.name: TypeMeta
fields.doc: Standard Kubernetes type metadata.

fields.name: Spec
fields.doc: Specification of the desired state of the PaddleJob.

fields.name: Status
fields.doc: Most recently observed status of the PaddleJob. Read-only (modified by the system).

api_types.name: PaddleJobSpec
api_types.doc: PaddleJobSpec is a desired state description of the PaddleJob.

fields.name: RunPolicy
fields.doc: RunPolicy encapsulates various runtime policies of the distributed training job, for example how to clean up resources and how long the job can stay active.

fields.name: ElasticPolicy
fields.doc: ElasticPolicy holds the elastic policy for paddle job.

fields.name: PaddleReplicaSpecs
fields.doc: A map of PaddleReplicaType (type) to ReplicaSpec (value). Specifies the Paddle cluster configuration. For example, { "Master": PaddleReplicaSpec, "Worker": PaddleReplicaSpec, }

fields.name: MinReplicas
fields.doc: minReplicas is the lower limit for the number of replicas to which the training job can scale down.  It defaults to null.

fields.name: MaxReplicas
fields.doc: upper limit for the number of pods that can be set by the autoscaler; cannot be smaller than MinReplicas, defaults to null.

fields.name: MaxRestarts
fields.doc: MaxRestarts is the limit for restart times of pods in elastic mode.

fields.name: Metrics
fields.doc: Metrics contains the specifications which are used to calculate the desired replica count (the maximum replica count across all metrics will be used).  The desired replica count is calculated with multiplying the ratio between the target value and the current value by the current number of pods. Ergo, metrics used must decrease as the pod count is increased, and vice-versa.  See the individual metric source types for more information about how each type of metric must respond. If not set, the HPA will not be created.

api_types.name: PyTorchJobList
api_types.doc: PyTorchJobList is a list of PyTorchJobs.

fields.name: TypeMeta
fields.doc: Standard type metadata.

fields.name: ListMeta
fields.doc: Standard list metadata.

fields.name: Items
fields.doc: List of PyTorchJobs.

api_types.name: PyTorchJob
api_types.doc: PyTorchJob Represents a PyTorchJob resource.

fields.name: TypeMeta
fields.doc: Standard Kubernetes type metadata.

fields.name: Spec
fields.doc: Specification of the desired state of the PyTorchJob.

fields.name: Status
fields.doc: Most recently observed status of the PyTorchJob. Read-only (modified by the system).

api_types.name: PyTorchJobSpec
api_types.doc: PyTorchJobSpec is a desired state description of the PyTorchJob.

fields.name: RunPolicy
fields.doc: RunPolicy encapsulates various runtime policies of the distributed training job, for example how to clean up resources and how long the job can stay active.

fields.name: PyTorchReplicaSpecs
fields.doc: A map of PyTorchReplicaType (type) to ReplicaSpec (value). Specifies the PyTorch cluster configuration. For example, { "Master": PyTorchReplicaSpec, "Worker": PyTorchReplicaSpec, }

fields.name: NprocPerNode
fields.doc: Number of workers per node; supported values: [auto, cpu, gpu, int]. For more, https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/blob/26f7f470df64d90e092081e39507e4ac751f55d6/torch/distributed/run.py#L629-L658. Defaults to auto.

fields.name: MinReplicas
fields.doc: minReplicas is the lower limit for the number of replicas to which the training job can scale down.  It defaults to null.

fields.name: MaxReplicas
fields.doc: upper limit for the number of pods that can be set by the autoscaler; cannot be smaller than MinReplicas, defaults to null.

fields.name: RDZVConf
fields.doc: RDZVConf contains additional rendezvous configuration (<key1>=<value1>,<key2>=<value2>,...).

fields.name: Standalone
fields.doc: Start a local standalone rendezvous backend that is represented by a C10d TCP store on port 29400. Useful when launching single-node, multi-worker job. If specified --rdzv_backend, --rdzv_endpoint, --rdzv_id are auto-assigned; any explicitly set values are ignored.

fields.name: NProcPerNode
fields.doc: Number of workers per node; supported values: [auto, cpu, gpu, int]. Deprecated: This API is deprecated in v1.7+ Use .spec.nprocPerNode instead.

fields.name: Metrics
fields.doc: Metrics contains the specifications which are used to calculate the desired replica count (the maximum replica count across all metrics will be used).  The desired replica count is calculated with multiplying the ratio between the target value and the current value by the current number of pods. Ergo, metrics used must decrease as the pod count is increased, and vice-versa.  See the individual metric source types for more information about how each type of metric must respond. If not set, the HPA will not be created.

fields.name: SuccessPolicy
fields.doc: SuccessPolicy defines the policy to mark the TFJob as succeeded. Default to "", using the default rules.

fields.name: TFReplicaSpecs
fields.doc: A map of TFReplicaType (type) to ReplicaSpec (value). Specifies the TF cluster configuration. For example, { "PS": ReplicaSpec, "Worker": ReplicaSpec, }

fields.name: EnableDynamicWorker
fields.doc: A switch to enable dynamic worker

summary: training-operator is a Kubernetes component. managing 6 CRD(s): JAXJob, MPIJob, PaddleJob, PyTorchJob, TFJob, XGBoostJob. deploying 3 workload(s) (training-operator, training-operator, training-operator) with 3 container(s). exposing 1 service(s). defining 7 API spec type(s) across 27 struct(s) with 110 total fields. with 4 operator config constants (2 image references). exposing 12 Prometheus metrics.
